canonical_json.encoding: UTF-8 without BOM, whitespace, or trailing newline
canonical_json.object_members: closed and sorted by Unicode code point
canonical_json.arrays: preserve declared semantic order unless a record explicitly declares sorting
canonical_json.strings: preserve raw Unicode scalar values without normalization; emit raw UTF-8 except JSON-required quote, reverse-solidus, and control escapes
canonical_json.u64: minimal unsigned decimal string; zero is exactly 0 and no leading zero is allowed
canonical_json.u32: JSON integer from 0 through 4294967295 with no fraction or exponent
canonical_json.f32: exact IEEE-754 bits as exactly eight lowercase hexadecimal characters
canonical_json.f64: exact IEEE-754 bits as exactly sixteen lowercase hexadecimal characters
canonical_json.numbers_forbidden: all JSON numbers except fields declared u32
canonical_json.non_finite: rejected by the owning domain type before encoding and after decoding

digests.exact_object: SHA-256 over exact stored bytes with no domain prefix; typed form sha256:<64-lowercase-hex>
digests.generation_preimage: domain || canonical-generation-byte-length as big-endian u64 || canonical-generation-bytes
digests.path_digest: the bare 64 lowercase hexadecimal characters from the applicable digest

ref.header: bytes 0..7 magic; 8..9 schema-version big-endian u16; 10 kind u8; 11 presence u8; 12..15 total-length big-endian u32
ref.project_uuid: bytes 16..31 in UUID network byte order
ref.current_digest: bytes 32..63 raw generation digest; always present
ref.previous_digest: bytes 64..95 raw generation digest or all zero when absent
ref.base_digest: bytes 96..127 raw manual-generation digest or all zero when absent
ref.checksum: bytes 128..159 raw SHA-256 defined by digests.ref_checksum_preimage
ref.path_kind_binding: refs/head=manual_head, refs/recovery=manual_recovery, refs/autosave-head=autosave_head, refs/autosave-recovery=autosave_recovery, and refs/pins/<checkpoint-id>=pin; swapping kinds is corruption
ref.unused_slots: every slot forbidden or absent for its kind is all zero; presence bits and the allowed per-kind slot combination must agree exactly

kinds.name: autosave_head
kinds.base: optional only for a provisional project; otherwise required

store_states.established: refs/head is present; refs/autosave-head, when present, has a required base equal to its current generation's base_manual_generation_id; comparison with refs/head.current classifies it as same-base or divergent
store_states.provisional: refs/head and refs/recovery are absent; refs/autosave-head is present with base absent, and its current autosave generation has base_manual_generation_id=null; every other combination without refs/head is corruption

ref.recovery_pair_rule: independently for the manual and autosave lanes: without a head, recovery is forbidden; with a head, recovery may be absent only when head.previous is absent, recovery.current may equal present head.previous for a clean pair, or recovery.current may equal head.current for an interrupted pre-head commit; the existing head.current remains authoritative and no ref is repaired automatically; any other recovery target is corruption

generation.generation_sequence: u64; initial zero and otherwise one greater than the maximum sequence in the current manual and autosave heads observed under the writer lease; diagnostic ordering only, never authority
generation.revision_high_water_sequence: u64 and not less than revision_sequence
generation.parent_generation_id: same-lane generation ID or null; provenance only and never a GC liveness edge
generation.base_manual_generation_id: required for autosave after a manual save, null for manual and provisional autosave
generation.forked_from: null or closed {project_id,generation_id}; fixed for the project and repeated unchanged in every generation created after Save As
generation.artifacts: the ProjectState artifact records in semantic order; every record contains one logical_object and one closed storage binding
generation.reachable_objects: the exact physical object closure derived from all artifact storage bindings, sorted by digest then byte length; duplicate digests are forbidden and no extra object is permitted

state_projection.optional_fields: dataset locator_hint/package_id/release_id and artifact derivation_id/recipe_id are always present and encode absence as JSON null

tagged_variant_shapes.render_dvr: closed {density_scale,mode,opacity_transfer,sampling} with mode=dvr; opacity_transfer is closed {curve,window}

state_projection.quaternion_restore: UnitQuaternion::from_canonical_xyzw preserves accepted f64 bits exactly

logical_object_binding.page_field_types: ordinal is u32; offset and byte_length are u64; digest is ExactBytesDigest
logical_object_binding.page_rules: pages are nonempty and sorted by ordinal; ordinal starts at zero, offset starts at zero, offsets are contiguous, every non-final page is exactly 16777216 bytes, the final page is 1 through 16777216 bytes, and concatenated length/digest equal logical_descriptor

direct.rule: object digest and byte length equal the artifact logical_object descriptor

paged.rule: media type and role equal this record's fixed logical-object-binding values

payload_storage.direct: a logical object of at most 16777216 bytes is stored once at its own ExactBytesDigest and its stored descriptor exactly equals the logical descriptor
payload_storage.paged: a logical object above 16777216 bytes is split at deterministic 16777216-byte offsets; pages and one canonical logical-object-binding manifest are stored as exact objects
payload_storage.paged_validation: page ordinals and offsets are contiguous from zero, every non-final page is exactly 16777216 bytes, page digests validate, concatenated bytes match the logical length and digest, and manifest logical facts equal the ProjectState RawObjectDescriptor
payload_storage.logical_binding_in_generation: each artifact record binds its logical descriptor to direct or paged storage; reachable_objects separately lists exactly every direct object or binding manifest and page object needed by those bindings

public_api_contract.actor: owns exactly one live session, every OS lease, the bounded request/completion queues, and the sole active filesystem transaction
public_api_contract.session: immutable safe metadata only: project ID, current manual/autosave generation IDs, effective open mode, and a display-safe package root; no lock or internal object paths
public_api_contract.writable_fallback: only prefer_writable may become an explicitly reported read_only session when the writer lease is unavailable
public_api_contract.commit_capture: immutable validated ProjectGenerationProjection plus expected lane parent, autosave base when applicable, fork provenance for Save As, and bounded lazy object sources
public_api_contract.object_source: opaque cancellable stream factory bound to one expected RawObjectDescriptor; no PathBuf, unbounded owned bytes, or preopened object collection
public_api_contract.save_as: caller supplies a projection with the new ProjectId and explicit source generation; the actor changes session only after durable destination installation and retains the old session on failure
public_api_contract.completion: every accepted request produces exactly one terminal completion carrying its ProjectStoreRequestId, including cancellation and an autosave superseded by coalescing
public_api_contract.receipt: opaque operation-specific anti-TOCTOU token; durable commits carry captured revision/high-water and new/current/previous generation IDs; autosave receipts also carry base manual generation and cannot mark a project saved
public_api_contract.recovery_candidate: bounded metadata only with generation, captured revision, origin, base/current relation, provisional/newer/stale/divergent classification, and recoverability summary; projection bytes load only after explicit selection

autosave_classification.provisional: no manual head exists and autosave base is absent
autosave_classification.divergent: a manual head exists and autosave base differs from manual head current
autosave_classification.newer: autosave base equals manual head current and autosave revision_sequence differs from manual head revision_sequence; revision IDs are immutable state identities, so undo may make the autosave sequence numerically lower
autosave_classification.stale: autosave base equals manual head current and autosave revision_sequence equals manual head revision_sequence

public_api_contract.diagnostics: bounded counters and stable reason codes only; no absolute/internal paths or raw OS error strings
public_api_contract.config: contains no filesystem-qualification bypass, hard-limit bypass, retry, compatibility, or alternate-path switch

transition_inventory.injection: each named transition supports before and after injection plus a zero-based occurrence for repeated writes, objects, pages, generations, refs, and GC moves
transition_inventory.commit_indeterminate: failure to establish final ref-directory durability after retry suspends writes, keeps the revision dirty, and requires close/reopen
transition_inventory.saved_revision_rule: only a durable manual-head completion may advance exactly its captured revision; autosave never advances saved_revision

fixture_manifest.required_cases: established newer autosave, stale same-base autosave, divergent autosave, provisional autosave-only project, exact manual/autosave recovery-to-previous pairing, positive recovery-ahead with and without head.previous, and unrelated same-lane recovery rejection
